mappings.rationale: Covers educational systems, policy formation, and the history of educational philosophies and institutions.
Covers the development and characteristics of higher education systems across different countries.
Covers Enlightenment educational theories and their influence on 18th-century educational reform (subsection B.6).
Covers Renaissance humanism and its influence on European educational development (subsection B.5).
Covers the impact of the Reformation and Counter-Reformation on education (subsection B.5.d).
Covers ancient Greek education as a foundational tradition in Western pedagogy (subsection B.1.d).
Covers ancient Roman education and its institutions (subsection B.1.e).
Covers medieval European education including monastic schools and the rise of universities (subsection B.3).
Covers the Confucian educational tradition in China, relevant to ancient Chinese and East Asian education (subsections B.1.b and B.4.b).
Covers the modernization of education in China, relevant to subsection B.8.c.
Covers Indian educational modernization including colonial and post-colonial developments (subsection B.4.a).
Covers the modernization of Japanese education from traditional to Western-influenced systems (subsection B.4.c).
Covers Islamic educational traditions and institutions (subsection B.2.d).
Covers Byzantine education influenced by Greek Christian and humanistic culture (subsection B.2.b).
Covers education in colonies and newly emerging nations as part of decolonization processes (subsection B.8.d).
Covers humanistic educational philosophy from the Renaissance through modern secular education.
Covers Rousseau's influential educational philosophy in Emile, relevant to 18th-century educational theory (subsection B.6.b).
Covers educational systems controlled by political parties and ideologically driven education (subsection A.3.d).
Covers sectarian education systems and Catholic educational traditions (subsection A.3.e).
Covers European educational influences in New World development (subsection B.6.c).
Covers Puritan educational philosophy and its influence on American educational development.
Covers the social setting of 19th-century educational reform driven by industrialization (subsection B.7.a).
Covers the spread of educational practices across cultures and the internationalization of education.
Covers theories of learning that shape educational practices and pedagogical methods.
Covers the historical development of attitudes toward children's education across different periods.
Covers the development of education in Africa including colonial and post-independence educational modernization (subsection B.8.d).
